summary: Joel Spolsky announces plans to expand his job board to serve the Indian market, noting strong readership from India but minimal job postings. Rather than making decisions unilaterally, he crowdsources advice from his readers on localization details including domain naming, pricing, payment methods, and scope. The post is essentially a public product requirements gathering exercise.
key_points: The Joel on Software job board has been successful but has almost no listings for India despite significant Indian readership
Spolsky openly crowdsources product decisions from his community rather than guessing at international market needs
Key localization concerns include domain naming conventions, payment methods beyond credit cards, pricing, and whether to include H1B visa sponsorship listings
money_quotes: The job board, jobs.joelonsoftware.com, has been rather successful.
I was thinking about setting up a separate job board specifically focused on jobs in India, since there are so many Joel on Software readers there and I've only seen three jobs (in Bangalore) posted.
What should I do to localize the job board for India?
key_insight: When expanding a product into an unfamiliar market, asking your existing users in that market what they need is more valuable than guessing.
